during_correctness_wins.name: locale_root_lowercase
during_correctness_wins.reason: Catalog type names are ASCII; default-locale lowercase folds Turkish 'I'/'İ' incorrectly. Pre-existing as TODO(kit) per PLAN.md DURING.
during_correctness_wins.helper: IdiomKit.lowercaseRoot (offered but not yet replacing direct call — direct call is idiomatic enough)

during_correctness_wins.name: locale_root_lowercase
during_correctness_wins.reason: View dialect strings ('trino', 'duckdb') compared case-insensitively; same Turkish-locale hazard.

during_correctness_wins.name: safe_sql_message
during_correctness_wins.reason: DuckDB JDBC SQLException.message is nullable on a handful of internal paths; previous code NPEs.

during_correctness_wins.name: safe_sql_message
during_correctness_wins.reason: Same NPE hazard; also drops the awkward `as java.lang.String` cast left over from a Java-Stream port.

during_correctness_wins.name: resource_leak_init
during_correctness_wins.before: init block opens connection then leaks it if createAppender throws
during_correctness_wins.after: try/catch closes app/conn in reverse construction order before rethrowing
during_correctness_wins.reason: Native DuckDB connection leak under SQLException during init; original Java had the same bug.

during_correctness_wins.name: resource_leak_finalize
during_correctness_wins.before: catch on SQLException only deletes the temp file; connection stays open
during_correctness_wins.after: best-effort connection.close() in the catch before propagating
during_correctness_wins.reason: Connection leak if appender.close or DETACH throws.

during_correctness_wins.name: resource_leak_parquet_writer
during_correctness_wins.before: parquetWriter.write then close, no try/finally — write throws → both writer and outputStream leak
during_correctness_wins.after: try block wraps write+close; catch closes the writer best-effort and rethrows
during_correctness_wins.reason: ParquetWriter closes its wrapped outputStream on close; a single best-effort close on exception suffices.

during_correctness_wins.name: retained_size_double_count
during_correctness_wins.reason: INSTANCE_SIZE already accounts for primitive fields (long, boolean). PLAN.md DURING.

during_correctness_wins.name: retained_size_double_count
during_correctness_wins.before: deleteFileFooterSizes keys counted via map AND deleteFilePaths list AND 4×SIZE_OF_LONG for the prim longs already in INSTANCE_SIZE
during_correctness_wins.after: map only counts values; list counts the strings once; primitive longs accounted by INSTANCE_SIZE
during_correctness_wins.reason: Same string is shared by reference between the list and the map's keys; INSTANCE_SIZE already includes the 4 primitive long fields.

idiom_cleanup.name: drop_requireNonNull_on_kotlin_non_null_params
idiom_cleanup.reason: Kotlin compiler emits Intrinsics.checkNotNullParameter for non-nullable params; requireNonNull is dead code. Kept ONLY where the parameter is T? (true null assertion).

idiom_cleanup.name: kotlin_collection_factories
idiom_cleanup.before: java.util.List.copyOf(x), java.util.List.of(), java.util.Map.copyOf(x), java.util.Set.copyOf(x), java.util.Map.of(), java.util.Set.of()
idiom_cleanup.after: x.toList(), emptyList(), x.toMap(), x.toSet(), emptyMap(), emptySet()
idiom_cleanup.reason: Kotlin built-ins are equivalent for defensive copies and empty constants; reads more naturally and drops the java.util.* import noise.
kept_at: java.util.List.of(arg) (with arguments) — left as-is to avoid subtly changing the returned type's identity

idiom_cleanup.name: convert_handles_to_jvm_record_data_class
idiom_cleanup.before: regular class with @JsonCreator constructor, @JsonProperty fun foo() accessors, manual equals/hashCode/toString, Objects.hash
idiom_cleanup.after: @JvmRecord data class with @get:JvmName / @param:JsonProperty on val params; auto-generated equals/hashCode; custom toString kept only where format diverges from data-class default (ColumnHandle, TableHandle, WritableTableHandle)
idiom_cleanup.call_sites_updated: ~270 .foo() → .foo across 16 main-source .kt files and 5 test .kt files
idiom_cleanup.rationale: Brings the 6 laggards in line with the existing precedent (DucklakeSplit, DucklakeMetadataTableHandle). Drops ~600 lines of boilerplate equals/hashCode/Objects.hash.
side_effects: DucklakeTableHandle.pushedExpressions and DucklakeWritableTableHandle.duckDbWriterMode were made strictly non-null (previously coerced null→default in init). Verified: all in-tree callers pass non-null values; Jackson always emits a list / a string. Wire-format compatibility unchanged.
Custom toString dropped from DucklakeMergeTableHandle (+DataFileRange), DucklakeInlinedSplit, DucklakeMetadataSplit — verified no tests grep their printed format.

idiom_cleanup.name: drop_jvmstatic_from_internal_companions
idiom_cleanup.rationale: The trino-ducklake plugin is now 100% Kotlin; @JvmStatic was defensive port noise. Kotlin call sites still use `ClassName.foo()` syntax identically with or without it.
kept_at: All SPI-surface classes (Trino plugin/Guice reflect on them): DucklakePlugin, DucklakeConnectorFactory, DucklakeConnector, DucklakeMetadata, DucklakeMetadataFactory, DucklakeSplitManager, DucklakePageSourceProvider, DucklakePageSourceProviderFactory, DucklakePageSinkProvider, DucklakeTransactionManager, DucklakeTransactionHandle, DucklakeSessionProperties, DucklakeTableProperties, DucklakeAlwaysOnSplitAffinityProvider, DucklakeAddFilesProcedure, DefaultDucklakeFileSystemFactory, DucklakeFileSystemFactory, DucklakeCatalogProvider, DucklakeModule.

All Jackson-serialized handles/splits: DucklakeColumnHandle (rowIdColumnHandle factory), DucklakeSplit, DucklakeTableHandle, DucklakeWritableTableHandle, DucklakeMergeTableHandle, DucklakeMetadataSplit, DucklakeInlinedSplit, DucklakeMetadataTableHandle, DucklakeMetadataTableType.
idiom_cleanup.rule: Drop @JvmStatic when EVERY caller is in this module AND no Java code (extension binary, generated, test fixture) imports the helper. Keep @JvmStatic on factory methods called via reflection (Trino plugin API).

not_applied.name: collapse_optional_to_nullable_on_internal_paths
not_applied.rationale: 203 Optional.empty/of/ofNullable occurrences across 26 .kt files. Many are SPI override return types (ConnectorMetadata.getInfo etc.) that must stay Optional. Many are Jackson-serialized field types on handles (e.g. DucklakeSplit.affinityKey: Optional<String>) that the catalog/wire format depends on.
not_applied.decision: Skipped in this slice. Splitting internal-only Optional<T> sites from SPI/wire sites requires per-call-site analysis — risk/value worse than the requireNonNull cleanup.

not_applied.name: move_companion_helpers_to_top_level
not_applied.rationale: Several files have a companion object whose body is only static helpers (e.g. DuckDbExpressionTranslator.translate*, DucklakeParquetSchemaBuilder.build*). Top-level `internal fun` is more idiomatic but the migration ripples through every call site (`ClassName.foo()` → `foo()`) and the import lists.
not_applied.decision: Deferred. Document the preference here; future authors should default to top-level fun for new helpers.

shared_helpers_introduced.name: IdiomKit.lowercaseRoot
shared_helpers_introduced.status: introduced, not yet rolled out to all callers (the two DURING fixes inlined the lowercase(Locale.ROOT) call directly because it's a one-liner)

shared_helpers_introduced.name: IdiomKit.safeMessage / firstLine / firstLineOf
shared_helpers_introduced.status: introduced, not yet rolled out to QuackDuckDbExecutor.firstLineOrFull / InProcessDuckDbExecutor warn path; those were fixed inline. Future PRs can collapse them to the kit helper.

verification.last_run: 2026-06-01 — passed after the handle-migration batch (DURING fixes + requireNonNull/copyOf cleanup + @JvmStatic removal + 6 handles → @JvmRecord data class + ~270 call-site updates). total=825 failures=0 errors=0 skipped=0.
